description_long: This is a classic biplane with a striking red fuselage and dual wings, each adorned with roundel insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The propeller is mounted at the nose, made of wood or wood-like material, and the engine cowling is painted in a metallic silver. The tail section features a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both matching the red color scheme. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, suitable for grass or dirt runways.
description: A red biplane with dual wings, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, featuring roundel insignias and a wooden propeller.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, roundel insignias, wooden propeller, and open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the open cockpit, and the fixed landing gear beneath the lower wing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer, as well as the dual wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the roundel insignias and tail markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
